resistance_model.note: The Beta (B) equation is an approximation valid within its 25-85 C fitting window; the datasheet R/R25 table is the reference for exact values, and the Beta result diverges by a few percent far from 25 C. In a build spec, the thermistor is emitted as an ordinary resistor whose <value> is the resistance at the temperature under test (compute R(T) from the law above, or read the datasheet R-T table). The 25 C point (R = R25 = 10 kOhm) is exact by definition.

source: Vishay NTCLE100E3103 datasheet (doc 29049), R25 = 10 kOhm +/-5%, B25/85 = 3977 K +/-0.75%. https://www.vishay.com/docs/29049/ntcle100.pdf
provenance_note: Behavioral, NOT a datasheet-exact thermal SPICE model. It is a parameterized resistor: the emitter produces a plain R card from <value>, and the divider math is what a build spec asserts. No self-heating, no thermal time constant, no temperature port -- temperature is chosen by the spec author as the <value> at that temperature.
